summary: McKenzie explains how high-ranking 'evergreen' blog posts can be strategically leveraged to bootstrap traffic for new products. He describes how his 'Free Bingo Cards' post ranks highly in search engines and generates 2,500 hits per month with zero ongoing promotion. By adding timely, topical content to the top of evergreen posts, he both refreshes their search rankings and redirects traffic to new projects like Daily Bingo Cards. The technique exploits the fact that users disproportionately click the first link in any content.
key_points: Evergreen blog posts gradually lose search rankings over time, but strategic updates can extend their shelf life and redirect their traffic value
Adding topical content to an evergreen post serves double duty: it signals freshness to search engines and creates a traffic funnel to new projects
The first link in any long piece of content gets the lion's share of clicks from both users and search engines
Bootstrapping a new product faces a chicken-and-egg problem — hard to get visitors without ranking, hard to get ranking without links, hard to get links without visitors
money_quotes: An 'evergreen' puts the lie to blogs being a medium which only covers breaking news and the controversies of the day — they keep producing value forever, typically by ranking highly for search terms of consequence.
Hard to get visitors without ranking, hard to get ranking without links, hard to get links without visitors — it's a vicious cycle.
In the Internet and in most economic activity, winners win.
The first link in any long bit of content gets the lion's share of the clicks.
Non-motivated traffic is worthless to you, and you're not developing the sort of repeat users that you want for your site(s).
key_insight: High-ranking evergreen content can be strategically refreshed with topical additions to both extend its search ranking lifespan and funnel traffic to new products.
